title: Timing Problem with make_tracing_sc example
body:
Overview: for make_tracing_sc verilator example: adding timing resolution of 1fs runs to completion
However, changing timing resolution to 10fs gives error:
Error Case Changes:
Makefile Added:
```
# Run Verilator in timing mode
VERILATOR_FLAGS += --timing
# Set default time resolution
VERILATOR_FLAGS += --timescale-override /10fs
```
In sc_main.cpp at beginning of sc_main() added:
```
    // define timing resolution as 1ps is way too large
    sc_set_time_resolution(10, SC_FS);
```
And in top.v and sub.v, just before the "module" lines added:
```
`timescale 1ps/10fs
```
running make clean and then make gives:
...
```
-- RUN ---------------------
obj_dir/Vtop +trace

        SystemC 2.3.4-Accellera --- Jul 24 2023 14:38:10
        Copyright (c) 1996-2022 by all Contributors,
        ALL RIGHTS RESERVED
%Error: SystemC's sc_set_time_resolution is 10^-99, which does not match Verilog timeprecision 10^-14. Suggest use 'sc_set_time_resolution(1s)', or Verilator '--timescale-override 1s/1s'
Aborting...
make: *** [Makefile:85: run] Aborted (core dumped)
> 
```
Case that works (I went back and forth quite a few times to verify):
Changes: exactly the same as the case that didn't work, but change all the "10"s to "1"s, that is use:
```
VERILATOR_FLAGS += --timescale-override /1fs
    sc_set_time_resolution(1, SC_FS);
`timescale 1ps/1fs
```
and example runs to completion:
...
```
[0] Model running...

Enabling waves into logs/vlt_dump.vcd...
[43000000] fastclk is 5 times faster than clk

*-* All Finished *-*
- sub.v:47: Verilog $finish

-- COVERAGE ----------------
/home/Dropbox/programming/Verilog/verilator/bin/verilator_coverage --annotate logs/annotated logs/coverage.dat
Total coverage (5/34) 14.00%
See lines with '%00' in logs/annotated

-- DONE --------------------
To see waveforms, open vlt_dump.vcd in a waveform viewer

> 
```
I can live with using a 1fs resolution (1ps is way too large), it just means I have redo my standard digital library; probably not a bit deal, but I still thought it might be of general interest to correct this.
p.s. I'm brand new to SystemC (and I don't really want to pay for the IEEE Reference Manual until I have to), but I don't think I made a mistake.
